title: ProofRecord
description: ExecutionProof authorization decision record with Ed25519 signature and validity-window semantics (ARK-455b)

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp of decision issuance. MUST carry an explicit UTC designator ('Z' or '+00:00'). This field is part of the JCS-canonicalized, Ed25519-signed payload AND is subject to a validity-window check at verification time (see notes.validity_window).

payload_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the governed payload (64-character hex string)

evidence_references.description: Array of URIs referencing supporting evidence for the decision

actor.description: Identity string of the decision-making authority or agent

execution_outcome.description: Actual execution result following the decision

review_path.description: Audit trail identifier for record review and compliance tracking

signature.description: Ed25519 signature (128-character hex string, 64 bytes). Present in signed records, absent during signing.

notes.canonicalization: RFC 8785 (JSON Canonicalization Scheme, JCS) before signing
notes.signature_algorithm: Ed25519 over the canonical byte representation of the 7 signed fields
notes.verification: TWO gates, both required to ACCEPT. Gate A: remove signature field, canonicalize remaining 7 fields via JCS, verify Ed25519 signature against canonical bytes. Gate B: parse the signature-verified timestamp as RFC 3339 UTC, compute age = verification_time - timestamp, REJECT if age < 0 (future) or age > ttl_seconds (expired).
notes.validity_window: A ProofRecord is valid only within [issuance_time, issuance_time + ttl_seconds]. Default ttl_seconds = 300. The timestamp is inside the signed payload, so a genuine post-signing edit breaks the signature (Gate A). A timestamp placed out of window BEFORE signing yields a valid signature but MUST still be rejected by Gate B. Expiry semantics follow ARK-442.
notes.change_from_v1.0: ARK-455 (schema v1.0) verified signature only. ARK-455b (schema v1.1) adds the mandatory validity-window gate and requires an explicit UTC designator on the timestamp.
